title: FormalJets
summary: Defines a purely formal expression language for metric jets, Taylor denominators, and obstruction coefficients.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines a purely formal expression language for metric jets, Taylor denominators, and obstruction coefficients.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Juhlformula/Juhlcombinatorics/FormalJets.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal expressions used in the Juhl obstruction recursion. Its constructors are: zero with arguments : Formal Expr; one with arguments : Formal Expr; rat with arguments : ℚ maps to Formal Expr; k with arguments : Formal Expr; f with arguments : Formal Expr; F with arguments : the natural numbers maps to Formal Expr; tr with arguments : the natural numbers maps to Formal Expr; add with arguments : Formal Expr maps to Formal Expr maps to Formal Expr; neg with arguments : Formal Expr maps to Formal Expr; mul with arguments : Formal Expr maps to Formal Expr maps to Formal Expr; op D with arguments : the natural numbers maps to Formal Expr maps to Formal Expr.
Rational scalar multiplication as syntax. In explicit terms, for q of type ℚ, and e of type formal expr, its defining expression is Formal Expr.mul Formal Expr.rat q E.
Rational factorial. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Nat.factorial n : ℚ.
1 / n! as a rational number. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is fact Q n ⁻¹.
The Taylor denominator 1 / (a! b!). In explicit terms, for a, b in the natural numbers, its defining expression is fact Q a times fact Q b ⁻¹.
Sum a list of formal expressions at the syntax level. In explicit terms, its defining expression is | maps to 0 | x :: xs maps to x plus list Sum xs.

Sum over 0,..., m at the syntax level. In explicit terms, for m in the natural numbers, and a of type the natural numbers maps to formal expr, its defining expression is list Sum List.range m plus 1.map A.
correspondence.leanType: def rangeSum (m : ℕ) (A : ℕ → FormalExpr) : FormalExpr

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Coefficient of the highest jet F_{m+1} in L_m. In explicit terms, for ell, m in the natural numbers, its defining expression is 4 times m plus 1 : ℚ minus ell : ℚ times inv Fact Q m.

The trace-times-radial-derivative part of L_m. In explicit terms, its defining expression is | 0 maps to 0 | m' plus 1 maps to range Sum m' the function sending j to Formal Expr.qsmul 2 times inv Fact2 Q j m' minus j Formal Expr.tr j times Formal Expr.F m' plus 1 minus j.

The formal Laplacian-variation part of L_m. In explicit terms, for m in the natural numbers, its defining expression is range Sum m the function sending j to Formal Expr.qsmul inv Fact2 Q j m minus j Formal Expr.op D j Formal Expr.F m minus j.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{deltaPart}(m)=\operatorname{rangeSum} m j \longmapsto \operatorname{FormalExpr}.\operatorname{qsmul} (\operatorname{invFact2Q} j (m - j)) (\operatorname{FormalExpr}.\operatorname{opD} j (F_{\mathrm{FormalExpr}} (m - j)))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The formal scalar factor k / 2 - ell. In explicit terms, for ell in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Formal Expr.qsmul 1 divided by 2 : ℚ Formal Expr.k plus Formal Expr.qsmul - ell : ℚ Formal Expr.one.

The zeroth-order trace part of L_m. In explicit terms, for ell, m in the natural numbers, its defining expression is k Over Two Minus Ell ell times range Sum m the function sending j to Formal Expr.qsmul inv Fact2 Q j m minus j Formal Expr.tr j times Formal Expr.F m minus j.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{traceZerothPart}(ell,m)=(\operatorname{kOverTwoMinusEll} \operatorname{ell}) \cdot (\operatorname{rangeSum} m j \longmapsto \operatorname{FormalExpr}.\operatorname{qsmul} (\operatorname{invFact2Q} j (m - j)) ((\operatorname{FormalExpr}.\operatorname{tr} j) \cdot (F_{\mathrm{FormalExpr}} (m - j))))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The part of L_m not containing the distinguished highest jet F_{m+1}. In explicit terms, for ell, m in the natural numbers, its defining expression is trace Derivative Part m plus delta Part m plus trace Zeroth Part ell m.
The full coefficient equation L_m. In explicit terms, for ell, m in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Formal Expr.qsmul highest Coeff ell m Formal Expr.F m plus 1 plus Lrest ell m.

references.locator: Formal Juhl-type formula and OR bridge
references.note: Primary source for the intended curved OR–Juhl identity.
Source for the Juhl side of the formal syntax.

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
